document.title: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: MDR Annex XIV, Part B – Post-market clinical follow-up
MDCG 2020-7 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Plan Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies, April 2020
MDCG 2020-8 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Evaluation Report Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies, April 2020
Class IIb in accordance with rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745

sourceSummary.oneLine: Manufacturer PMCF evaluation report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under MDR-related technical documentation.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents PMCF activities, findings, conclusions, and impact on clinical evaluation, risk management, and technical documentation.
keyPoints: The report states that PMCF is a continuous process under Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and that the evaluation report is part of the clinical evaluation report and technical documentation.
The device is described as wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, except infants.
The report identifies no additional impact on clinical safety and performance through PMCF activities and no need for preventive or corrective measures.
The report references MDR Annex XIV Part B, MDCG 2020-7, and MDCG 2020-8.

concepts.evidence: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.

evidence.claim: The activities were conducted as planned and there were no deviations from the PMCF plan.

evidence.claim: The PMCF found no new critical issues regarding device performance or safety.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal indicates CE/MDR, but the source text identifies MDR references and an authorised representative rather than a notified body, competent authority, CE certificate, or formal approval authority.
Activities summary contains placeholder counts such as 'X related Survey' and 'X related Literature', so exact collected-data counts are not fully extractable from that sentence.
Minor OCR or text extraction artifacts are present, including broken words such as 'there w ere' and checkbox symbols, but key document metadata remains readable.
